description: Attempted baseline run with an unsupported run_experiment command wrapper; rerunning the harness directly.

asi.hypothesis: Use the repo-root autoresearch harness as the benchmark entrypoint.
asi.rollback_reason: run_experiment rejected the wrapped command and required ./autoresearch.sh directly.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun with command './autoresearch.sh' so the benchmark and checks execute normally.

description: Baseline render benchmark with the new autoresearch harness on the default linux case.

asi.hypothesis: Capture a stable end-to-end baseline before changing the render path.
asi.observed_effect: constructAndRender median is ~120.3ms while isolated renderStaticWindow is ~127.0ms, so the current benchmark still spends most time in work that survives into the render-only pass or is affected by measurement noise.
likely_hotspots: per-row SSR cost for the first window
asi.next_action_hint: Probe whether Root/useTree/core can avoid constructing full visible item arrays or full item instances when virtualization only needs a bounded window.

description: Deferred full item-instance materialization until lookup time and rendered virtualized rows from cached visible item IDs instead of tree.getItems(). Big render win, but internal core tests failed on eager instanceBuilder expectations during rebuilds.

asi.hypothesis: Virtualization should not need a full item instance array for the whole visible tree. Cache visible IDs/meta in core, lazily create item instances on demand, and render rows from the cached ID list.
asi.observed_effect: Very large primary-metric improvement (~52% better than baseline).
asi.rollback_reason: packages/trees/test/core/core.test.ts asserts that rebuildTree and expanded-state config changes trigger instanceBuilder calls. The lazy refactor removed those eager calls.
failing_checks: core/feature-composition > Synchronous Data Loader > rebuilds item instance > rebuilds when explicitly invoked
core/feature-composition > Synchronous Data Loader > rebuilds item instance > rebuilds when config changes with new expanded items
core/feature-composition > Proxified Synchronous Data Loader > rebuilds item instance > rebuilds when explicitly invoked
core/feature-composition > Proxified Synchronous Data Loader > rebuilds item instance > rebuilds when config changes with new expanded items
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect whether eager instanceBuilder calls are a real internal contract. If yes, preserve those rebuild-time side effects while still letting Root/virtualized render paths consume cached visible IDs without calling tree.getItems(). If not, update the internal tests and re-validate the broader suite carefully.

description: Cached visible item IDs during rebuilds and rendered virtualized rows from that cached ID list instead of forcing tree.getItems() to materialize the full visible item instance array. Preserved rebuild-time root instance creation so core instanceBuilder expectations still hold.

asi.hypothesis: The virtualized render path only needs row IDs/counts up front, not eagerly built item instances for every visible node. Cache visible IDs in core, then create item instances lazily per rendered row while still refreshing the root instance on rebuild to preserve internal builder semantics.
asi.observed_effect: Large confirmed win: construct_and_render_ms dropped from ~120.3ms baseline to ~58.2ms with checks passing.
implementation_notes: create-tree rebuild now records visible IDs and delays full item-instance array materialization until getItems()
Root.tsx and benchmark runtime now slice/filter visible IDs instead of calling tree.getItems() in the virtualized path
rebuildTree still rebuilds the synthetic root instance each time to satisfy core instanceBuilder expectations
asi.next_action_hint: Now inspect the remaining full-tree work before virtualization: path/id map construction, expanded-path mapping, and any avoidable per-row ancestor/idToPath work in Root and TreeItem.

description: Added phase timing instrumentation directly to the render benchmark to isolate fresh-load costs. It revealed the next hotspots, but the instrumentation itself regressed the primary metric enough that it should not stay in the measured benchmark path.

asi.hypothesis: Instrument the fresh constructAndRender path itself so the benchmark reports which internal stages still dominate after the lazy visible-ID win.
asi.observed_effect: Useful breakdown: fileListToTree ~37.6ms, buildIdMaps ~8.7ms, useTreeStateConfig ~5.6ms, useTree ~7.3ms, visible-item selection ~0ms, tree-item prop prep ~0.04ms.
asi.rollback_reason: The instrumentation overhead raised construct_and_render_ms from ~58.2ms to ~61.1ms, so the measured benchmark should stay clean.
asi.next_action_hint: Optimize real hot stages without benchmark overhead: first investigate removing or reusing the extra path/id map pass and trimming useTreeStateConfig's expanded-path mapping for the all-folders-expanded initial render. Re-add phase timing later behind an opt-in debug flag if needed.

description: Tried caching path->id lookups on fileListToTree output and reusing that map in Root/benchmark runtime so fresh render would skip rebuilding pathToId. This regressed the primary metric.

asi.hypothesis: fileListToTree already computes the final hashed IDs, so attaching a cached path->id map there and reusing it in Root should be cheaper than rebuilding pathToId on the fresh render path.
asi.observed_effect: Construct-and-render regressed from ~58.2ms to ~64.0ms with checks still passing.
asi.rollback_reason: The extra Map population inside hashTreeKeys appears to cost more than the later Root-side map rebuild it removed. Saving one pass in Root did not offset extra work added to every fileListToTree result.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pay additional work inside fileListToTree just to save a later pass. Focus next on cheaper Root/useTreeStateConfig changes or on a closer client-mount benchmark that captures the real docs-page fresh-render path.

name: Optimize trees fresh client mount render

description: Established the first client-mount baseline on the new benchmark harness. The benchmark ran at ~76.8ms, but checks failed because the new client benchmark script left an unused DEFAULT_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT constant that breaks tsc.

asi.hypothesis: Use the new jsdom client-mount harness as the primary autoresearch target and capture a clean baseline before further optimizations.
asi.observed_effect: client_mount_ms baseline on the current tree is ~76.8ms with checksum guards intact.
asi.rollback_reason: packages/trees/scripts/benchmarkVirtualizedFileTreeClientMount.ts declares DEFAULT_VIEWPORT_HEIGHT but never reads it, so trees tsc fails in autoresearch.checks.sh.
asi.next_action_hint: Recreate the client benchmark harness with the unused constant removed, rerun the baseline so the new target is on a kept commit, then profile Root/useTreeStateConfig/client mount setup for the next win.

description: Kept the new jsdom client-mount benchmark harness as the primary autoresearch target, wired it into trees scripts/autoresearch.sh, and fixed its tsc issue. The fresh-mount baseline on the linux fixture is now ~73.9ms with checks passing.

asi.hypothesis: A dedicated jsdom client-mount benchmark will better track the real docs-page fresh render path than the older SSR-only benchmark, and landing it cleanly gives a trustworthy baseline for the next optimization loop.
asi.observed_effect: The new primary baseline is client_mount_ms ~73.9ms with stable checksum guards on the linux fixture.
implementation_notes: added a smoke test for the new benchmark CLI
wired benchmark:render:client into packages/trees/package.json and autoresearch.sh
removed the unused constant that previously broke trees tsc
asi.next_action_hint: Profile the remaining fresh-mount path inside Root/useTreeStateConfig/useTree. Likely candidates are full-tree path/id map construction and expanded-path mapping before virtualization chooses the first visible rows.
